outcome_contract.why: Guests can be sent links to activity events that appear to end after exactly 2 hours regardless of how long the activity actually runs. Staff have no way to adjust this without a code change. Adding a duration field to the staff activity form means new activities will display accurate end times.
outcome_contract.intended_outcome_statement: Staff can create and edit activity instances via the Prime app UI, setting a custom duration in minutes. Activity cards show accurate end times derived from data, not a hardcoded default.

engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: Staff form at `/owner/activities`; list + create + edit; design system components
Loading/saving/error/success states in form; optimistic update optional

engineering_coverage.applicable: Requires Firebase rules permitting client writes from staff auth; rules not versioned
engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: Validates staff session at function layer; service account write; no client rule change needed
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: Staff session validated server-side before any write; aligns with existing function auth pattern

engineering_coverage.applicable: Client write has no audit log unless manually added
engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: Function layer can emit structured log + telemetry event on create/edit
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: Add `direct-telemetry` event from function on successful write; provides audit trail

engineering_coverage.applicable: Client write in Jest needs Firebase emulator or mock; achievable but harder
engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: Function is a plain async handler; easy to unit-test with mock `FirebaseRest`
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: Mock `FirebaseRest.set()` in Jest; assert correct path + payload

engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: Same — validate payload against type before write
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: Server function validates `durationMinutes >= 1`, required fields present; returns error 400 otherwise
New pages/function additive; rollback = revert form to redirect stub

stage_payload.decision_summary: Staff cannot set custom durations on activity instances in the Prime guest app without directly editing Firebase RTDB via the console. `durationMinutes` is already defined in the `ActivityInstance` type and RTDB schema, but is optional with a silent 120-minute hardcoded fallback. No admin UI or API endpoint exists for activity CRUD. The decision is which approach to use to expose duration management to staff.
goals: Staff can create and edit activity instances (including `durationMinutes`) through an in-app UI.
Activity cards display accurate end times derived from data, not the silent 2-hour default.
non_goals: Full activity management dashboard with analytics or bulk operations.
constraints: Activity management must be staff-only; must not be accessible from the guest session.
Firebase RTDB is the persistence layer.
Cloudflare Pages Functions (`functions/api/`) are the established server-side layer for privileged operations.
assumptions: `PRIME_FIREBASE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_EMAIL` and `PRIME_FIREBASE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_PRIVATE_KEY` are set in the CF Pages environment (same env vars used by `aggregate-kpis.ts`).
`durationMinutes` remains optional in the type for backward compatibility with existing records.

evaluation_criteria.Why it matters: Write access to `messaging/activities/instances` is a privileged operation; must be gated to authenticated staff only
Modifying Firebase security rules is a console-only operation, not versioned in repo; changes carry a risk of misconfiguration
Plan scope should stay bounded; both options must be achievable in one small plan
The write path must be unit-testable without a live Firebase connection
New code should follow existing patterns to minimize maintenance surface

options_considered.Description: Staff form in owner area writes directly to Firebase RTDB via the browser client SDK (same pattern as presence marking)
options_considered.Upside: Simpler implementation; no new Cloudflare function needed
options_considered.Downside: Client writes require Firebase security rules covering `messaging/activities/instances` specifically. The presence write pattern (`messaging/activities/presence`) confirms rules exist for some paths; coverage of the `instances` path is unconfirmed and unversioned in repo
options_considered.Key risks: Rules for `instances` may not permit client writes; misconfiguration could expose RTDB to broader writes

options_considered.Description: New `functions/api/activity-manage.ts` validates staff session, then writes to RTDB via `FirebaseRest.set()` using service account credentials
options_considered.Upside: Privileged write stays server-side; same pattern as `aggregate-kpis.ts`; no Firebase security rule change needed for client-side; fully unit-testable
options_considered.Downside: One additional file (function) + form must `fetch()` to endpoint
options_considered.Key risks: Service account env vars must be set (already required for aggregate-kpis)

options_considered.Description: Make `durationMinutes` required in type; document Firebase console process
options_considered.Downside: Does not solve the operational problem; staff still need Firebase console access

chosen_approach.Recommendation: Option B — Server-side Cloudflare Function for RTDB writes.
chosen_approach.**Option A (client-side direct write)**: requires unversioned Firebase security rule changes in the console; unclear if current rules allow it; risk of misconfiguration. The server-side approach is marginally more code but much safer.
chosen_approach.**Option C (type tightening + docs)** —: does not give staff any UI. The operational problem (no in-app creation flow) remains unsolved. Type tightening alone would break any existing Firebase records that lack `durationMinutes`.

rejected_approaches: **Option A (client-side direct write)** — Viable but rejected: requires unversioned Firebase security rule changes in the console; unclear if current rules allow it; risk of misconfiguration. The server-side approach is marginally more code but much safer.

**Option C (type tightening + docs)** — Rejected: does not give staff any UI. The operational problem (no in-app creation flow) remains unsolved. Type tightening alone would break any existing Firebase records that lack `durationMinutes`.

sequencing_constraints: Function (server-side write) before form integration — form depends on the function for real responses.
Form component and owner page scaffold can be built in parallel with the function (using a mock endpoint); integration is the final step.
Owner activities page depends on the form component.
Redirect is independent; can be TASK-01.

areas.label: Staff creates an activity
areas.current: Staff uses Firebase console → manual JSON
areas.future: Staff fills form (title, startTime, durationMinutes ≥ 1, status, optional: description/meetUpPoint/meetUpTime) → POSTs to `POST /api/activity-manage` → function validates staff session + payload → `FirebaseRest.set()` writes to `messaging/activities/instances/[newId]` → function returns 200 → form shows success
areas.steady: Guest-facing read path unchanged; `ChatProvider` real-time listener already picks up new instances automatically
areas.seams: Service account env vars must be verified in CF Pages; planning must include wrangler.toml secrets confirmation

areas.label: Staff edits an activity
areas.current: Staff uses Firebase console → direct JSON edit
areas.trigger: Staff navigates to `/owner/activities` → taps "Edit" on an existing instance
areas.future: Form pre-populated with existing data → staff edits → POSTs to `PATCH /api/activity-manage` → function validates session + payload → `FirebaseRest.update()` on existing path → 200
areas.steady: Guest-facing real-time subscription propagates update automatically
areas.seams: Edit must not allow changing `id`; function must reject id mismatch

areas.label: Guest views activity
areas.future: Same rendering — no change. For instances created via new form, `durationMinutes` will be present; guest sees accurate end time

areas.label: `/chat/activities/manage` route
areas.seams: Ensure redirect doesn't break any existing staff bookmark
